accurate.why: All claims match the bill text and verified context: EO 14323 declared July 30, 2025, imposed duties on Brazilian imports, terminated via NEA section 202.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; the description of the NEA section 202 procedure and the emergency's purpose is accurate context.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the resolution terminates the national emergency underlying the Brazil import duties.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly identifies the National Emergencies Act (50 U.S.C. 1622) and the joint-resolution termination mechanism.

exceptions_conditions.why: The bill contains no exceptions or conditions, and the summary correctly notes their absence.

effective_dates.why: The bill specifies no effective date or sunset; summary correctly states termination occurs upon enactment with no delay.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures the sole operative effect: terminating the declared national emergency under EO 14323.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and descriptive with no editorializing.

coherent.why: The summary is well-organized, consistent, and clearly written.

concise.why: Brief and free of padding, though slightly repetitive in restating termination.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names EO 14323, the National Emergencies Act, and section 202 (50 U.S.C. 1622).

correct_figures.why: The dates and citations stated (July 30, 2025; section 202; 50 U.S.C. 1622) are all correct.
